name: HEPATIC ADENOMA
definition: HEPATOCELLULAR ADENOMA = LIVER CELL ADENOMA epithelial neoplasm composed of normal hepatocytes TELANGIECTATIC FOCAL NODULAR HYPERPLASIA = TELANGIECTATIC ADENOMA usually incidentally discovered tumor (multiple in about 50%) occurring exclusively in rare condition with > 10 adenomas in both hepatic lobes without history of steroid therapy /

demographics.Prevalence: half as common as FNH; increasing with duration of oral contraceptive use + size
demographics.Path: spherical benign growth with high incidence of hemorrhage + necrosis + fatty change;
demographics.Histo: hepatocytes arranged in sheets without acinar structure (no portal or central vein),
demographics.Peak age: 40 years; young women in childbearing age (90%); not seen in males unless on
demographics.Location: right lobe of liver in subcapsular location (75%); multiple in up to 21%
demographics.Age: young woman with a history of oral contraceptive usage; obese patient
demographics.Genetics: nonfunctioning tumor suppressor gene located on chromosome 12q24 ← biallelic
demographics.Associated with: male hormone administration, glycogen storage disease, familial

dominant_finding: mass of decreased density ← gross fat (7-10%) + areas of necrosis (30-40%)
distribution: right lobe of liver in subcapsular location (75%); multiple in up to 21%
discriminating_features: homogeneous signal drop-off (86% sensitive, 100% specific) ← intracellular steatosis
frequently heterogeneous ← dilated sinusoids, blood-filled (pelioid) spaces, focal necrosis,

general: frequently heterogeneous ← dilated sinusoids, blood-filled (pelioid) spaces, focal necrosis,
unusual "nodule-in-nodule" appearance in large tumors (DDx: hepatocellular carcinoma)
CT: mass of decreased density ← gross fat (7-10%) + areas of necrosis (30-40%)
hyperdense areas of fresh intratumoral hemorrhage (15-50%)
CECT: transient avid homogeneous enhancement on arterial-phase images in small adenomas
iso- / hypoattenuating on portal venous + delayed-phase images
US: usually small well-demarcated nonlobulated solid heterogeneous mass of variable
hyperechoic lesion with well-defined hypoechoic rim in normal surrounding liver
echogenic in areas of fat or hemorrhage
hypoechoic in background of diffuse fatty infiltration
central vessels with triphasic pattern / continuous flat venous waveform
arterial vascularity with centripetal filling + peripheral rim of sustained enhancement +
iso- to moderately increased vascularity + mixed filling during arterial phase +

higher signal intensity in periphery of lesion ← dilated sinusoids
